issues.title: Allow `MenuAnchor` created menus to traverse the submenu via keyboard.
issues.body:
Currently, if you create a menu using `MenuAnchor`, and you tab to the button, you can activate it, but you can't traverse to the submenu using the keyboard.  This is because the submenu is in the overlay, and the button isn't handling the key events.

I'm not quite sure what the fix for this is, because it may be that the button itself has to add a shortcut for the arrow and tab keys to get to the submenu. Or maybe it just needs to focus the first item in the submenu when activated.
comments:
Hi @gspencergoog,
There is a reproduction case at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/144381 which pointed out that only `Tab` key doesn't work on `SubmenuButton`. I see `Arrow` keys still works. I wonder if this issue assemble that case?
The `triaged-desktop` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-desktop` label or `fyi-desktop` label.
So I actually think this issue is distinct from https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/144381, but is the same as another issue I filed here: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/147770.

I've just bumped into this issue as well (`Flutter 3.22.3 • channel stable • Framework • revision b0850beeb2 (3 weeks ago) • 2024-07-16 21:43:41 -0700`).

I have a column of menu anchors.  When you press the up arrow (when the menu is above the anchor), you expect to enter the menu, or you expect to already have the menu in focus (as Spencer mentioned).  Instead, the focus moves up to the previous menu anchor in the list.

> There is a reproduction case at #144381 which pointed out that only `Tab` key doesn't work on `SubmenuButton`. I see `Arrow` keys still works. I wonder if this issue assemble that case?

When tabbing into the anchor and then activating the menu, tab no longer performs any action.  I can still use the arrows to navigate the column of anchors , though "arrow-ing" off the anchor leaves its menu open.

Weirdly, I found that by setting the `MenuAnchor.childFocusNode` I can consistently get into the menu by pressing the right arrow (and only the right arrow), which selects the first menu item.
@PvtPuddles Can you post an example and/or try it on master? I think some of the focus changes I PR'd have landed on master.

Your workaround works beautifully on the stable branch, all issues addressed.

I am eager for the remaining fixes to make their way through the system and I look forward to updates.

Thank you.
Glad it helped! Yeah, hopefully the coming changes will make this workaround unnecessary -- we'll see soon.

Currently it works correctly on all platforms but Web, where down arrows doesn't produce any effects. This is because on other platforms, the down arrow is processed by the `Shortcuts` in `WidgetsApp`, where both `_defaultShortcuts` and `_defaultAppleOsShortcuts` dispatch a `DirectionalFocusIntent` for arrow keys. On Web, `_defaultWebShortcuts` dispatches `ScrollIntent` for arrow keys.

In short, it's the `DirectionalFocusIntent` that matters, no matter which widget dispatches it, and currently their dispatching has nothing to do with `MenuAnchor`s (although their processing does.)

issues.title: [TextField] Odd behavior with Microsoft IME Japanese Text input method
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

When activating Japanese IME text input on Windows 10, with any input method different from Alphanumeric (e.g. Hiragana), the ```TextField``` widget behaves oddly.

After the first few characters typed into the ```TextField```, the system automatically underlines them, which is expected as it does the same on Notepad.

If I change the cursor position by clicking on another character, every character input duplicates what is preceded by it and prepends it to the current string.

The attached video gives a much better idea of I'm trying to say.



### Expected results

I expected to just insert the new characters without duplication.

### Actual results

It duplicated the inserted string.

### Code sample

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  // This widget is the root of your application.
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'IME Bug Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: const MyHomePage(title: 'IME Bug Demo'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {

  final String title;

  @override
  State<MyHomePage> createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  final TextEditingController textEditingController = TextEditingController();

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: Text(widget.title),
      ),
      body: Center(
        child: Column(
          mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
          children: <Widget>[

}
```

@huycozy A few more points to emphasize the difference and the priority:

1. > But when there is maxLines: null, the spell check doesn't work.

No, it still works. We found no pure-Dart way to disable the spell check in Firefox. This is the screen of @yescorp with `maxLines: null`:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/44893228/223448848-d8808946-8158-47f6-b77f-f88d9e5a08f9.png)

Here is `maxLines: 3`:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/44893228/223449279-0972cfd4-ae87-4da3-9808-50d34b055f3e.png)

3. #40682 is to make the spellcheck work while we currently need to disable the compulsory spellcheck, so it is different.

4. A huge factor in this issue is inconsistency. From the docs on the `spellcheck` attribute of `textarea`: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Global_attributes/spellcheck
> If this attribute is not set, its default value is element-type and browser-defined.

This leads to spellcheck being disabled in Chrome and enabled in Firefox.

I believe the proper way of solving this to be:

1. Unify the current behavior in different browsers as the fastest thing to do. I believe the least surprising way is to set `spellcheck="false"` on `textarea`s to match Chrome which is the market leader.
2. Make `spellcheck` attribute configurable with `TextField.spellCheckConfiguration` (which it is not now according to the test above). This is what #40682 was likely supposed to mean.

Also, As I observed the behavior of the browser, the `<textarea>` tag doesn't appear immediately. It only appears when you click on the `TextField`.

This may be useful when resolving the issue.
`spellCheckConfiguration` should not have any effect on the web, since on the web spell checking is implicit. There's no API for it. But it looks like a real bug.

CC @camsim99

Did we ever do anything to enable spell check on web?  I thought that it was disabled (even built-in spell check).

https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Global_attributes/spellcheck
@justinmc We didn't do anything, but we did see that built-in spell check worked when @yjbanov enabled it!
Ah ok, so that has been enabled by default on web now?

> Ah ok, so that has been enabled by default on web now?

No no, he just tried it experimentally.

Got it, thanks for jogging my memory!

So then @alexeyinkin diagnosed the problem correctly above in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/122092#issuecomment-1458282146.  Firefox enables spell checking [by default](https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/how-do-i-use-firefox-spell-checker#:~:text=Firefox%20automatically%20checks%20the%20spelling,words%20in%20the%20installed%20dictionary.).  There is no way to disable it in Flutter right now short of maybe writing a plugin to set the HTML attribute.

Long term, the solution is probably that Flutter *should* control the `spellcheck` attribute using SpellCheckConfiguration.

issues.title: Input always move the cursor at end of text selection
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new project with basic template with `flutter create input_test`.
2. Paste the code below at `Code sample` section.

3. Scroll to the end of page, select any word and try to move the select cursor over the text above looks like on the video attached.

### Expected results

1. When selection moves the scroll position in reverse mode (moving to top on this case) the cursor should be placed on the first character of selection, avoiding this strange scroll flick.
2. Otherwise, the cursor must be placed at the last character of selection (seems correct in this case).

### Actual results

When selection moves the scroll position in reverse mode (moving to top on this case) the cursor always retorn to the last character of input, causing a strange scroll, especially when the distance between the start and end of selection is very large or has reached the top

### Code sample

issues.title: [Flutter Driver] The driver.tap() method right after application startup sometimes fails to open the keyboard on Android
issues.body:
### Type of Request
Bug

### Environment
Running the `Linux_pixel_7pro integration_ui_keyboard_resize` test on an Android device with the Flutter Driver

- Application under test: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/dev/integration_tests/ui/lib/keyboard_resize.dart
- Test: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/dev/integration_tests/ui/test_driver/keyboard_resize_test.dart

The goal of the test is: "Ensure keyboard dismissal resizes the view to original size" (eg not directly related to this bug)

### What is happening?

Depending on the device executing the test and the device running the test, the following happens infrequently (but frequently enough to make the test flaky)


1. The test launches the keyboard_resize test application using Flutter Driver in `enableTextEntryEmulation: false` mode
2. The test waits for the `defaultTextField` TextField to be available via `await driver.waitFor(defaultTextField);`
3. The test taps on the `defaultTextField` TextField via `await driver.tap(defaultTextField);`
5. The `defaultTextField` TextField gets focus
6. The Android keyboard is not opening. The test fails as it needs the keyboard open to proceed. The logs contain a warning from InputManager about not opening the keyboard:

```
W/InputMethodManager(20089): Ignoring showSoftInput() as view=io.flutter.embedding.android.FlutterView{636da98 VFE...... .F....ID 0,0-1080,2277 #1} is not served.
```
Expected: InputMethodManager opens the keyboard.

Video of this happening (runs the tests multiple times, fails at the last one): https://drive.google.com/file/d/1dQmIPs7mCVjxocCvWB9KSfsaSiB7_Tyh/view?usp=sharing 

Easily reproducible on MacBook Pro M1 + Pixel 8 Pro API 36. Also, with Pixel 9 Pro XL. 
In LUCY, it is happening on Pixel 7 Pro (API unknown)
Does not happen or happens very rarely on older Android devices. Not reproducible with Pixel 3.

### Reproduce

NOTE: If the workaround in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/162308 is already merged, then the test will not fail anymore, but logs will show the retries. If you prefer to debug with failure, then move the `await driver.tap(defaultTextField);` or use this infinite loop setup with additional logs: https://github.com/harri35/flutter/blob/fe80784cddb7dfdad1538cff26161abd34ec5e01/dev/integration_tests/ui/test_driver/keyboard_resize_test.dart#L41

1.  **Disable automatic reboots:** Comment out the `await checkForRebootRequired();` line in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/9e273d5e6e2e75336f26951a09aba0f5656a7e77/dev/devicelab/lib/framework/framework.dart#L240-L243
2.  **Disable automatic retries:** Set `static const int retryNumber = 0;` in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/9e273d5e6e2e75336f26951a09aba0f5656a7e77/dev/devicelab/lib/framework/cocoon.dart#L56-L57
3.  **Install `hyperfine`:**  This is a command-line benchmarking tool (e.g., `brew install hyperfine` on macOS).
4.  **Connect a device/emulator:** Connect a newer physical Android device. Use a direct cable between the computer and device, skip any slow USB hubs.
5.  **Navigate to devicelab:** Navigate to the flutter\dev\devicelab\ directory
6.  **Run repeated tests:** Use `hyperfine` to run the test multiple times and capture the output:    
```bash
    hyperfine -r 2000 --style=full 'dart bin/test_runner.dart test -t integration_ui_keyboard_resize >> log.txt'
```
7. **Observe the logs:**: Look for the `W/InputMethodManager(...): Ignoring showSoftInput() ..`


Note: If you use the setup with additional logs, then this command is useful on Mac:

`grep -wo -F -i -E "(No|[0-9]+) retries were used to make this PASS\." log.txt | sort | uniq -c`


### Additional details

When running the test 2000x times, the following patterns can be observed.

Iterations it takes to open the keyboard if infinite retries are allowed:

MacBook Pro M1 + Pixel 8 Pro API 36 - at 2000/2000 successful tests
```
  569x "No retries were used to make this PASS."
 1428x "1 retries were used to make this PASS."
    1x "31 retries were used to make this PASS."
    1x "53 retries were used to make this PASS."
    1x "54 retries were used to make this PASS."
```

Logs have the following pattern - the keyboard can only be opened after the `D/InsetsController(...): hide(ime(), fromIme=false)` appears in the log. 

...
<-Test starts->
`.. end-to-end test Ensure keyboard dismissal resizes the view to original size ..`
<- All attempts to open the keyboard fail in this period ->
`D/InsetsController(...): hide(ime(), fromIme=false)`
<- All attempts to open the keyboard succeed now ->
`I/ImeTracker(...): com.yourcompany.integration_ui:...: onCancelled at PHASE_CLIENT_ALREADY_HIDDEN`
`I/ImeTracker(...): com.yourcompany.integration_ui:...: onRequestShow at ORIGIN_CLIENT reason SHOW_SOFT_INPUT fromUser false`
`D/InsetsController(12023): show(ime(), fromIme=false)`
<- Keyboard opens somewhere here .. ->
`D/InsetsController(12023): Setting requestedVisibleTypes to -1 (was -9)`
`D/InputConnectionAdaptor(12023): The input method toggled cursor monitoring on`
...

Looking a the timing, 
- In the tests with no retries or 1 retry the `D/InsetsController(...): hide(ime(), fromIme=false)` takes ~ 1 second to appear from start of the test
- In the test with 54 retries it takes 21 seconds to appear

The `D/InsetsController(...): hide(ime(), fromIme=false)` log seems to be coming from https://cs.android.com/android/platform/superproject/main/+/main:frameworks/base/core/java/android/view/InsetsController.java;l=1208

Note: Using waitForTappable makes no difference.

### All background and investigation

For all details and info, see

- https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/162308#discussion_r1950970463
- https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/161300

issues.title: TextField as WidgetSpan mixes parent TextField initial text when delete or arrow keys used
comments: Thank you for the detailed report! I can reproduce this on Flutter master version `3.27.0-1.0.pre.573` as well.

Thanks huycozy.  I've also tested on a physical ipad (pro, m1) with an Apple ipad magic keyboard attached and have the same problem as the ios simulator (delete key works, arrow keys cause the problem, software delete key also works fine and touch selection works), so definitely effects mobile too.
CC @Renzo-Olivares from triage.

Any thoughts on this?  I've looked through the code myself to see if I could spot any obvious fix but gets to a point which is outside of my experience level.

Thanks

Hi @doug9876 thanks for reporting this issue. I think part of this issue is currently working as intended, it looks like your custom controller sets an initial text value, but overrides what is actually rendered by `RenderEditable` with a new `TextSpan` tree in `buildTextSpan`. The issue is that the `TextInputPlugin` in the engine is unaware of this override and the result of `buildTextSpan`, and is only aware of the raw text value you set on the controller `Initial text in parent text field`. So when you try to use the arrows to move within the parent field, the framework and engine are actually using `Initial text in parent text field` as the value its traversing and not the result of `buildTextSpan`. You can see this clearly if you change the initial text to an empty string, you will not be able to traverse the parent field with arrows. We haven't yet implemented a way for the engine to be aware of `WidgetSpan`s and their concrete lengths.

With regards to the behavior of the delete and arrow keys in the nested `TextField`s, the `TextField`s focus logic and related components have not been wired up to supported nested `TextField`s. 

Is there a specific use-case you are trying to accomplish by placing a `TextField` within another `TextField`?
CC @Renzo-Olivares from triage to follow up.

Hi,

Any thoughts on whether this can be fixed, or workarounds other than using an overlay?

Thanks

issues.title: FormField validators are invoked with incorrect/old values
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

Created a test file that explains what I get and what I expect. Can be found here: https://gist.github.com/daniel-v/f1133f3e39a5714a3774760dab3841b2

**In short**

I'd expect that when I call `FormState.validate()` in response to a `TextEditingController` notification, the validators would receive the value  of the `TextEditingController`. This isn't the case, it seems that `FormState` caches a value, which seems to be updated AFTER validators would run. FormState's value is passed to the validators, therefore validators are always receive a value that's old.

**Question**

Is that expected and is this how this should work?

**Repro**

```
flutter create repro_proj
cd repro_proj
curl https://gist.githubusercontent.com/daniel-v/f1133f3e39a5714a3774760dab3841b2/raw/f41c63228c5bfaadbd56210582613706d095856c/input_validation_test.dart -o test/form_validator_test.dart
```

comments: I observed that setting **auto_validate = true** in Form leads to the test being passed

@mannprerak2 thank you for taking the time checking it out!

You are right, when `autovalidate` is true, the test pass, however that is a different use case. Imagine having more than a single field where you want to trigger certain events on form value change (such as enabling/disabling a submit button) but only if the form is valid. `autovalidate` and `onChange` combo has different side effects: 

1. `onChange` on form seems to be triggered as many times as the number of fields 
2. I'd still have to invoke `FormState.validate()` as it seems to be the only mechanic I could find to check if there are validation errors. Validation also forces a rebuild. Together with no. 1 it starts to be a "meh" workaround

A "livable" workaround I found is to use `scheduleMicrotask` in the `TextEditingController`'s listener callback to wait for the value of TextFormField to be updated.

``` 
    final tFormApp = newFormApp(fieldValidator);

    return Form(
      key: _formKey,
      child: TextFormField(
``` 

issues.title: Up and down arrow keys miscalculate the cursor position in a TextField
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- You must include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Run `flutter create bug`.

2. Update `main.dart` as follows:
```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() async {
  runApp(MaterialApp(home: Material(child: TextFieldTest())));
}

class TextFieldTest extends StatelessWidget {
  @override

    return const TextField(
      minLines: 1,
      maxLines: 12,
      style: TextStyle(fontSize: 40),
    );
  }
}
```

3.

* Run the app using chrome/canvaskit
* Type the following into the textfield `aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa`
* Select a character in the middle and use the up/down arrow keys

It seems to depend on the font size. For example, with the html renderer, setting the font size to something small like 14 seems to trigger the bug, while a fontSize of 40 on html works fine.


**Expected results:**
I expect the cursor to the character directly above or below. This behavior works perfectly in MacOS.

**Actual results:**
It ends up jumping to the left/right as well.

Here is a video of it in action:

```dart
 LogicalKeySet(LogicalKeyboardKey.arrowUp): const MoveSelectionUpTextIntent(),
    // const DoNothingAndStopPropagationTextIntent(),    
```

The code for `renderEditable.moveSelectionUp(...)` seems to properly calculate cursor positions on html/canvaskit.

issues.title: Selection toolbar/handler should disappear when it is opened and user clicks outside of it
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce - Issue /1

1. Run the code bellow as mobile-web (or go to this deployed website: https://epic-thompson-badb9e.netlify.app/#/).
2. Select text in the text_field/selectable_text.
3. Tap outside of the text_field/selectable_text

**Expected results:** The selection toolbar/handler should disappear.
**Actual results:** Selection toolbar/handler is still visible and the only way to remove it is to select one of the options in the toolbar.

Note: while reproducing you can notice some unwanted behavior: double tap select text but not show selection toolbar. Let's focus on the actual issue.

![gif](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/33294549/101696283-308c8700-3a76-11eb-947c-efdfa4cb1556.gif)


## Side Note - issue /2

About the selection toolbar not disappearing reproduces on `stable` and on `master` channel. The toolbar not disappearing on mobile apps does not follow the Material Design rules: https://material.io/design/platform-guidance/android-text-selection-toolbar.html which says:
![Screen Shot 2020-12-09 at 23 51 56](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/33294549/101698323-929abb80-3a79-11eb-9601-ae3b10f96a37.png)


1. Run the code bellow.
2. Select text in the text_field/selectable_text.
3. Tap outside of the text_field/selectable_text

**Expected results:** The selection toolbar should disappear.
**Actual results:** Selection toolbar is still visible and the only way to remove it is to select one of the options in the toolbar.

![gif](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/33294549/101697157-94637f80-3a77-11eb-81b6-9d2320464851.gif)


<details><summary>Flutter Code</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(SampleWidget());

class SampleWidget extends StatelessWidget {
  @override

    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: Center(
            child: Column(
              mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min,
              children: [
                TextField(
                  controller: TextEditingController(text: 'Flutter is great\nChange my mind.'),
                  minLines: 3,
                  maxLines: 5,

                Padding(
                  padding: const EdgeInsets.all(32),
                  child: SelectableText("Flutter is great. Change my mind."),

}

I am planning to unblock fixes for text editing inconsistencies like this through an upcoming refactor of EditableText ([design doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1QaVIr1bbOWJGNyyNsB75sXgTDRMuxP268sLGTBNDut0/edit), [first PR](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/75032)).

I can reproduce the issue on the latest `master`.

### Investigation 
Since `SelectionOverlay` [uses](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/57aa49028db77a4edf0fbc6efc70cceea2d2c6ed/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/text_selection.dart#L592) `Overlay`, it is not dismissable like a dialog pointed in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/72029#issuecomment-858761123

Currently selecting one of the items from toolbar [dismisses](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/57aa49028db77a4edf0fbc6efc70cceea2d2c6ed/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/text_selection.dart#L1619) selection overlay (which hides toolbar) and `SelectionOverlay` only [takes](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/57aa49028db77a4edf0fbc6efc70cceea2d2c6ed/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/text_selection.dart#L954-L996) the space it needs to host toolbar and provide it at a specific location so tapping outside the toolbar does nothing. 

I tried to provide a Container to `SelectionOverlay` and wrap it with `GestureDetector`, which provides an area that is tappable outside the toolbar and calling `hide()`  onTap hides selection overlay when tapping outside the toolbar but this doesn't seem like a good solution as it breaks other tests (even after targeting just mobile).

    • Engine revision 0155f8fc45

    • DevTools version 2.40.0-dev.1

```

</details>

Thank you for the analysis @TahaTesser! It's reassuring that this only happens in an Overlay at least. I want to look at this but likely won't get a chance for a week or two. I will assign myself if I'm working on it.

I think the root cause here is the fact that TextSelectionOverlay was never meant to have its values updated, and so we simply dispose and recreate it whenever they do:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/b397ee3297e46adabc28a91c3fc76cd2c34c213f/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L3075-L3076

In this case, the OverlayEntries are built and then immediately disposed, and when initState is called on their _OverlayEntryWidgetStates, their OverlayEntry has already been disposed, causing the error `Null check operator used on a null value`.

Really, we should come up with a way to update these values that doesn't involved disposing and recreating the overlay.

CC @LongCatIsLooong in case you happen to have any ideas or think that there is something to fix in Overlay. Otherwise I think everyone should use the workaround in the meantime until we can do a bigger refactor of TextSelectionOverlay.

Duplicate of #24214

This is easily repro'd by making a scaffold with appbar, making a listview in the body with a textfield. get the cursor on the textfield and scroll down the page and then it will scroll into the appbar. Native android makes the cursor disappear. gist example here: https://gist.github.com/seanfreiburg/d7d9ccf3cf8f355dd2c4efe9ad264953 Also happens on bottom nav bar

I think the issue the handles are an Overlay, which gives it priority to show over the app bar. This means that the AppBar needs to be "over" the overlay or maybe the handles should be a Stack instead of an Overlay.

@zoechi I dont think this is a duplicate of #24214 , the issue is similar but not the same. That bug is that the markers go outside the editable text viewport, this is an issue of the markers not staying in the body of the scaffold. The Overlay is writing outside the scope of widgets that it should. @sjindel-google can you confirm this is not the same issue? I saw you made the fix for the markers escaping left and right here https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/24476

@seanfreiburg It's not the same issue. The logic there is about explicitly hiding and fading the handles when the EditableText knows they're outside the bounds of the text. This is a higher-level issue, which I wouldn't expect can be fixed in such a way.

In addition to showing on top of the app bar (the original report above), the selection handles can also paint over:
* the status area at the very top of the screen on iOS (@darshankawar's repro above at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/13182#issuecomment-1333584879 )
* a bottom app bar (@seanfreiburg's report https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/13182#issuecomment-504173245 above)
* the background above the top edge of a modal bottom sheet (@luanemanuel's report #128905)

Presumably all of these symptoms are related to the selection handles being an overlay and getting painted atop most of the rest of the app, along the lines @seanfreiburg suggested above:

> I think the issue the handles are an Overlay, which gives it priority to show over the app bar. This means that the AppBar needs to be "over" the overlay or maybe the handles should be a Stack instead of an Overlay.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8036ed5c-6ec5-4c5c-a7b3-8e031bece703

Here a sample of the bug in action.
Still not fixed? 😞
I'm having the same issue. Is there any way to fix it? Please!

Hi! I'd like to work on this issue if no one is currently working on it.

I've been doing some local experiments following the same approach used in #138313 (context menu fade on scroll) —listening to parent `ScrollNotification` via `ScrollNotificationObserver` in `EditableText` and toggling handlesVisible based on whether the selection bounds are within ancestor viewports.

I've had some promising results and could dedicate time this week to polish it and open a PR. Let me know if this approach sounds reasonable or if there's anything I should consider.

The fix is still rough — the hide timing needs tuning (handles disappear a bit too late) and re-showing on scroll back needs work. But the core mechanism is in place. Here's a quick video of the current state: 


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9d9a835f-6085-49d8-aba4-f5be0bf34fed

One key question about any solution will be how it behaves in all the different cases that have been described in this thread. See https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/13182#issuecomment-1639418992 and https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/13182#issuecomment-2103499338 for a total of 5 different situations.
